source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - H2S Gas Release, Main Pass 299-FP Platform

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - H2S Gas Release
actions_taken: H2S alarm activated and platform shut in
All personnel mustered to primary station
Response team inspected H2S sensors #81 and #83
Response team located gas leak in 2-inch pipe outlet from MBF-935 Buyback Gas Scrubber
2-inch outlet piping of MBF-935 Buyback Gas Scrubber replaced on 05-June-2016
event.summary: On 29-May-2016 at 1310 hrs, a hydrogen sulfide (H2S) gas release of 400 ppm occurred on Main Pass 299-FP Platform during pigging operations on the flowline from MP 299-A to FP Platform. While the crew drained fluids from the pig trap, H2S sensors detected high concentrations of sour gas. The H2S alarm sounded and the platform was shut in. All personnel donned SCBAs and mustered. After an initial all-clear declaration, the H2S alarm sounded again. The response team located a gas leak in the 2-inch pipe outlet from the MBF-935 Buyback Gas Scrubber below the south side cellar deck. Investigation determined the leak was caused by extensive exterior corrosion that compromised the piping integrity. The root cause was identified as failure of the operator to perform routine corrosion assessment and maintenance on piping. The field was subsequently shut in indefinitely as continued operation was deemed uneconomical.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District made no recommendations to the Office of Incident Investigations

threats.name: Piping Corrosion Due to Lack of Routine Maintenance
Exterior Corrosion and Pitting of Vent Piping

controls.name: Routine Corrosion Assessment and Maintenance Program
controls.barrier_role: Prevent piping corrosion and loss of containment
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance is the primary defense against corrosion-induced piping failure

supporting_text: Failure of the Operator to perform routine corrosion assessment maintenance on piping was the primary cause of this incident
The condition of the piping showed evidence of a lack of routine maintenance
The corrosion that allowed the H2S to leak was a direct result of a lack of routine piping maintenance on the facility

controls.name: H2S Detection System (Sensors #81 and #83)
controls.barrier_role: Detect H2S release and trigger alarm response
controls.lod_basis: Detection system provides early warning of H2S release to enable emergency response

supporting_text: The H2S sensors at #81 and #83 located near the pig trap and the gas MBF-935 Buyback Gas Scrubber skid, were 'amber light' activated
Then the H2S high alarm sounded indicating a release of sour gas
the high H2S alarm sounded again, activated by the H2S sensor #81 located in the buyback skid

controls.name: Platform Automatic Shutdown System
controls.barrier_role: Isolate platform and prevent continued release upon H2S detection
controls.lod_basis: Automatic shutdown isolates the source and prevents escalation of the incident

supporting_text: the H2S high alarm sounded indicating a release of sour gas and the safety systems then shut in the platform

controls.name: Personnel Muster and SCBA Deployment
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from H2S exposure during incident response
controls.lod_basis: SCBA provides respiratory protection to enable safe personnel evacuation and response

supporting_text: All personnel onboard mustered to the primary station where they donned the Safe-Contained Breathing Air (SCBA) and waited for further instructions
The response team (RT) that included the H2S Safety Burner Tech, the Field Lead Operator and the Deck Lead Operator masked up with voice active SCBAs and went to inspect both H2S sensors #81 and #83
The crew donned a new set of SCBAs to ensure there was enough safe breathing air to make it back downstairs to the safe breathing air manifold

controls.name: H2S Monitoring Panel and Field Foreman Reporting
controls.barrier_role: Monitor H2S levels and communicate status to personnel
controls.lod_basis: Real-time monitoring and communication enable informed decision-making during incident response

supporting_text: the Field Foreman was verbally reporting the H2S parts per million (PPM) readings for sensors #81 and #83

the Field Foreman notified the crew that the H2S, as measured at sensors #81 and #83, had dissipated to a safe level according to H2S panel readings. He therefore declared an 'all-clear' of the H2S high level
Shortly afterwards, as the crew were taking off their SCBA packs, the high H2S alarm sounded again

controls.name: Portable H2S Gas Detectors for Area Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Locate source of H2S release and verify safe conditions
controls.lod_basis: Portable detectors enable response team to identify leak location and assess area safety

supporting_text: The RT began using gas H2S detectors to check the area around sensors #81 and #83 to discover the source of the leak
The RT used gas detectors to again check the area around the MBF-935 Buyback Gas Scrubber. During this check the RT found a gas leak in 2-inch pipe outlet coming from the MBF-935 Buyback Gas Scrubber

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
